question_title: Will the interest in “armenia” change between 2026-02-17 and 2026-02-28 according to Google Trends?
summary: Armenia search interest is elevated at ~86 (rescaled) on Feb 17, driven by the historic JD Vance visit to Armenia on Feb 9-11. The resolution compares rescaled values within the Jan 29-Feb 28 window. Mean reversion from this elevated level strongly favors "Decreases" (AR(1) model: 89%, bootstrap: 42%). The "no change" band is structurally very narrow (~5 raw points) due to the rescaling mechanism. A blended model gives ~60% Decreases, ~30% Increases (driven by possibility of new catalysts from elections/diplomacy), and ~10% Doesn't change.

factors.description: Feb 17 value elevated ~16 points above baseline (~55) due to Vance visit spike; mean reversion strongly favors decline over 11 days
AR(1) model predicts 89% probability of decrease given strong mean-reversion dynamics in this series
Second bump on Feb 16-17 (68, up from 50 on Feb 14) suggests possible sustained or renewed interest, weakening pure decay narrative
Ongoing Armenia news catalysts: June 2026 elections, church crisis, nuclear agreements, Iran partnership could sustain or increase interest
Rescaling mechanism makes 'no change' band extremely narrow (~5 raw points out of 100), structurally suppressing middle option
Empirical change_stats base rates show 31.5% increases, 27.2% decreases, 41.3% no change for daily transitions - but 11-day window has higher variance

reasoning:
I'll start by gathering data on this question.Let me get more data and search for Armenia-related news through other channels.Now let me analyze the data more carefully. The 3-month daily data shows important patterns. Let me also search for upcoming Armenia events.Now let me run a quantitative analysis of the volatility and project forward.Now let me save my findings and write the meta-reflection.**Forecast: Decreases (60%), Increases (30%), Doesn't change (10%)**

**Key reasoning:**

1. **Elevated starting point favors decline**: The Feb 17 value of 68 (3-month scale) is ~16 points above the baseline of ~55, driven by VP Vance's historic visit to Armenia on Feb 9-11. Mean reversion over 11 days strongly favors a decrease.

2. **Rescaling mechanics compress "no change"**: In the resolution range (Jan 29-Feb 28), Feb 17 rescales to ~86 (with peak at 100 on Feb 10). The "no change" band requires the raw Feb 28 value to fall in a very narrow 65-70 range — only ~5 points wide. Both Monte Carlo models agree on ~7-10% for this outcome.

3. **Monte Carlo results**: The AR(1) model (mean-reverting) gives 89% Decreases; the bootstrap model (random walk from current level) gives 42% Decreases, 51% Increases. I blend these at 40/60 weighting, giving ~60% Decreases.

4. **Upside catalysts exist**: Armenian parliamentary elections (June 2026), ongoing church crisis, nuclear cooperation deals, and Iran partnership discussions could sustain interest. A second bump on Feb 16-17 shows some resilience. This keeps P(Increases) meaningful at 30%.
